revision.text:
{{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = 倭文氏 |画像 = |氏姓 = 倭文[[宿禰]]（大和）
倭文[[連]]（摂津） |始祖 = [[天羽槌雄神]] |出自 = |氏祖 = [[大味宿禰]]（大和）
[[天伊佐布魂命]]（摂津） |種別 = [[神別]]（天神） |本貫 = [[大和国]]、[[摂津国]]ほか |人物 = |後裔 = }} '''倭文氏'''（しどり/しずり-うじ）は、「倭文」を[[氏#古代氏族としての「氏」|氏]]の名とする[[氏族]]。異表記は「委文」。 == 概要 == 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』（[[815年]]）によると、「[[大和国]][[神別]]（天神）」の項に「委文[[宿禰]]　出自[[カミムスビ|神魂命]]之後[[大味宿禰]]也」とあり、また「[[摂津国]][[神別]]（天神）」の項に「委文[[連]]　[[角凝魂命]]男[[伊佐布魂命]]之後也」とある。 機織の神である[[天羽槌雄神]]を[[祖神]]として奉斎し、全国に[[倭文神社]]が残る。 {{Notelist}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title=社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-|work=家紋World|accessdate=2020-01-22|ref=shake}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title=倭文氏と下照姫　倭文神社（２）|work=蘇える出雲王朝|accessdate=2020-01-22|ref=izumo}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title=天羽槌雄神|work=玄松子の祭神記|accessdate=2020-01-22|ref=izumo}} == 関連項目 == * [[倭文神社]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しとりうし}} [[Category:氏]] [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[Category:近畿地方の氏族]]

{{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = 倭文氏 |画像 = |氏姓 = 倭文[[宿禰]]（大和・河内）
倭文[[連]]（摂津） |始祖 = [[天羽槌雄神]] |出自 = |氏祖 = [[大味宿禰]]（大和）
[[天伊佐布魂命]]（摂津） |種別 = [[神別]]（[[天津神・国津神|天神]]） |本貫 = [[大和国]]、[[河内国]]、[[摂津国]]ほか |人物 = |後裔 = }} '''倭文氏'''（しとり/しどり/しずり-うじ）は、「倭文」を[[氏#古代氏族としての「氏」|氏]]の名とする[[氏族]]。異表記は「委文」。 == 概要 == [[織物]]を生産する[[部民制|部民]]（べみん）である倭文部（しとりべ）を率いた[[伴造]]（とものみやつこ）氏族[[#ujizoku|日本古代氏族事典]]－「しとり［委文］」。倭文とはシズオリの意で、[[アサ|麻]]や[[カジノキ|穀（かじ）]]などの繊維で文様を織り出した日本古来の織物[[#kodaishi|日本古代史大辞典]]－「しどり［倭文］」。 中央の一族は[[連]]（むらじ）の[[カバネ|姓（かばね）]]であり、その中の主流の一族は[[天武天皇]]13年（[[684年]]）に[[宿禰]]姓を賜った。地方の伴造には、[[連]]（むらじ）、[[臣]]（おみ）、[[首 (姓)|首]]（おびと）などの姓がある。 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』（[[815年]]）によると、[[大和国]]と[[河内国]]に委文宿禰、[[摂津国]]に委文連の三氏を掲げている。出自としては、「大和国[[神別]]（[[天津神・国津神|天神]]）」の項に「委文宿禰　出自[[カミムスビ|神魂命]]之後[[大味宿禰]]也」とあり、また「摂津国神別（天神）」の項に「委文連　[[角凝魂命]]男[[伊佐布魂命]]之後也」とある[[#izumo|倭文氏と下照姫　倭文神社（２）]]。 [[織機|機織]]の神である[[天羽槌雄神]]を[[祖神]]として奉斎し、全国に[[倭文神社]]が残る。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Notelist}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{citation|和書|editor=[[佐伯有清]]|title=日本古代氏族事典【新装版】|publisher=[[雄山閣]]|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|和書|editor=上田正昭：監／井上満郎・愛宕元：|title=日本古代史大辞典|publisher=大和書房|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title=倭文氏と下照姫　倭文神社（２）|work=蘇える出雲王朝|accessdate=2020-01-22|ref=izumo}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title=社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-|work=家紋World|accessdate=2020-01-22|ref=shake}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title=天羽槌雄神|work=玄松子の祭神記|accessdate=2020-01-22|ref=izumo}} == 関連項目 == * [[倭文神社]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しとりうし}} [[Category:氏]] [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[Category:近畿地方の氏族]]

{{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = 倭文氏 |画像 = |氏姓 = 倭文[[宿禰]]（大和・河内）
倭文[[連]]（摂津） |始祖 = [[天羽槌雄神]] |出自 = |氏祖 = [[大味宿禰]]（大和）
[[天伊佐布魂命]]（摂津） |種別 = [[神別]]（[[天津神・国津神|天神]]） |本貫 = [[大和国]]、[[河内国]]、[[摂津国]]ほか |人物 = |後裔 = }} '''倭文氏'''（しとりうじ、しどりうじ、しずりうじ）は、「倭文」を[[氏#古代氏族としての「氏」|氏]]の名とする[[氏族]]。異表記は「委文」。 == 概要 == [[織物]]を生産する[[部民制|部民]]である倭文部（しとりべ）を率いた[[伴造]]氏族[[#ujizoku|日本古代氏族事典]]－「しとり［委文］」。倭文とはシズオリの意で、[[アサ]]や[[カジノキ]]などの繊維で文様を織り出した日本古来の織物[[#kodaishi|日本古代史大辞典]]－「しどり［倭文］」。 中央の一族は[[連]]の姓（[[カバネ]]）であり、その中の主流の一族は[[天武天皇]]13年（[[684年]]）に[[宿禰]]姓を賜った。地方の伴造には、[[連]]、[[臣]]、[[首 (姓)|首]]などの姓がある。 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』（[[815年]]）によると、[[大和国]]と[[河内国]]に委文宿禰、[[摂津国]]に委文連の三氏を掲げている。出自としては、「大和国[[神別]]（[[天津神・国津神|天神]]）」の項に「委文宿禰　出自[[カミムスビ|神魂命]]之後[[大味宿禰]]也」とあり、また「摂津国神別（天神）」の項に「委文連　[[角凝魂命]]男[[伊佐布魂命]]之後也」とある[[#izumo|倭文氏と下照姫　倭文神社（２）]]。 [[織機|機織]]の神である[[天羽槌雄神]]を[[祖神]]として奉斎し、全国に[[倭文神社]]が残る。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Notelist}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{citation|和書|editor=[[佐伯有清]]|title=日本古代氏族事典【新装版】|publisher=[[雄山閣]]|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|和書|editor=上田正昭：監／井上満郎・愛宕元：|title=日本古代史大辞典|publisher=大和書房|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title=倭文氏と下照姫　倭文神社（２）|work=蘇える出雲王朝|accessdate=2020-01-22|ref=izumo}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title=社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-|work=家紋World|accessdate=2020-01-22|ref=shake}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title=天羽槌雄神|work=玄松子の祭神記|accessdate=2020-01-22|ref=izumo}} == 関連項目 == * [[倭文神社]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しとりうし}} [[Category:氏]] [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[Category:近畿地方の氏族]]

倭文[[連]]（摂津） |始祖 = [[天羽槌雄神]] |出自 = |氏祖 = [[大味宿禰]]（大和）
[[天伊佐布魂命]]（摂津） |種別 = [[神別]]（[[天津神・国津神|天神]]） |本貫 = [[大和国]]、[[河内国]]、[[摂津国]]ほか |人物 = |後裔 = }} '''倭文氏'''（しとりうじ、しどりうじ、しずりうじ）は、「倭文」を[[氏#古代氏族としての「氏」|氏]]の名とする[[氏族]]。異表記は「委文」。 == 概要 == [[織物]]を生産する[[部民制|部民]]である倭文部（しとりべ）を率いた[[伴造]]氏族[[#ujizoku|日本古代氏族事典]]－「しとり［委文］」。倭文とはシズオリの意で、[[アサ]]や[[カジノキ]]などの繊維で文様を織り出した日本古来の織物[[#kodaishi|日本古代史大辞典]]－「しどり［倭文］」。 中央の一族は[[連]]の姓（[[カバネ]]）であり、その中の主流の一族は[[天武天皇]]13年（[[684年]]）に[[宿禰]]姓を賜った。地方の伴造には、[[連]]、[[臣]]、[[首 (姓)|首]]などの姓がある。 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』（[[815年]]）によると、[[大和国]]と[[河内国]]に委文宿禰、[[摂津国]]に委文連の三氏を掲げている。出自としては、「大和国[[神別]]（[[天津神・国津神|天神]]）」の項に「委文宿禰　出自[[カミムスビ|神魂命]]之後[[大味宿禰]]也」とあり、また「摂津国神別（天神）」の項に「委文連　[[角凝魂命]]男[[伊佐布魂命]]之後也」とある[[#izumo|倭文氏と下照姫　倭文神社（２）]]。 [[織機|機織]]の神である[[天羽槌雄神]]を[[祖神]]として奉斎し、全国に[[倭文神社]]が残る。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Notelist}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{citation|和書|editor=[[佐伯有清]]|title=日本古代氏族事典【新装版】|publisher=[[雄山閣]]|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|和書|editor=上田正昭：監／井上満郎・愛宕元：|title=日本古代史大辞典|publisher=大和書房|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title=倭文氏と下照姫　倭文神社（２）|work=蘇える出雲王朝|accessdate=2020-01-22|ref=izumo}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title=社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-|work=家紋World|accessdate=2020-01-22|ref=shake}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title=天羽槌雄神|work=玄松子の祭神記|accessdate=2020-01-22|ref=izumo}} == 関連項目 == * [[倭文神社]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しとりうし}} [[Category:氏]] [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[Category:近畿地方の氏族]]

revision.comment: Created page with "'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori). These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神}}'''..."
revision.text:
'''Shidori Shrine''' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine '''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto}}''' (also known as '''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto''', '''Ameno Hachio''', or '''Takehatsuchi''') as the '''deity of weaving''', alongside '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神}}''' (also known as '''Amano Yachichihime''' or '''Amanoorihime'''). These shrines were established by the '''{{ill|Shidori clan|ja|倭文氏}}''', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The '''origin of these shrines is attributed to''' the '''{{ill|Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社}}''' in '''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture'''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to '''{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}''', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of '''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}'''. Therefore, these shrines provide '''important historical evidence''' regarding '''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).'''
Additionally, in the '''2016 animated film "Your Name"''', the fictional '''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社}}''' is depicted as enshrining '''Shidori deity Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto'''.
== Major Shidori Shrines ==
The following shrines are listed in the '''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}''':
* '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Nanba District, Kōzuke Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Isesaki)|ja|倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)}} (Higashi-Kaminomiya-cho, {{ill|Isesaki, Gunma|ja|伊勢崎市}}, [[Gunma Prefecture]]) * '''Shizuka Shrine (静神社)''' in '''Kuji District, Hitachi Province''' – {{ill|Shizuka Shrine|ja|静神社}} ({{ill|Naka, Ibaraki|ja|那珂市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Koma District, Kai Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nirasaki)|ja|倭文神社 (韮崎市)}} (Miyakubo, {{ill|Nirasaki, Yamanashi|ja|韮崎市}}, [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Fuji District, Suruga Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|倭文神社 (富士宮市)}} (Hoshiyama, {{ill|Fujinomiya, Shizuoka|ja|富士宮市}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Tagata District, Izu Province''' – Identified with multiple candidate shrines, including '''{{ill|Kuwado Shrine|ja|鍬戸神社}}''' ({{ill|Mishima, Shizuoka|ja|三島市}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Suzuka District, Ise Province''' – '''{{ill|Kasado Shrine|ja|加佐登神社}}''' (merged into [[Suzuka, Mie|鈴鹿市]], [[Mie Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Kasa District, Tango Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Maizuru)|ja|倭文神社 (舞鶴市)}} (Imada, {{ill|Maizuru, Kyoto|ja|舞鶴市}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Yosa District, Tango Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yosano)|ja|倭文神社 (与謝野町)}} (Mikawachi, {{ill|Yosano, Kyoto|ja|与謝野町}}, [[Kyoto Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Asago District, Tajima Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Asago)|ja|倭文神社 (朝来市)}} (Maruyama, {{ill|Asago, Hyogo|ja|朝来市}}, [[Hyogo Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Takakusa District, Inaba Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tottori)|ja|倭文神社 (鳥取市)}} (Shidori, {{ill|Tottori, Tottori|ja|鳥取市}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Kawamura District, Hōki Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Yurihama)|ja|倭文神社 (湯梨浜町)}} (Miyauchi, {{ill|Yurihama, Tottori|ja|湯梨浜町}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Kume District, Hōki Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Kurayoshi)|ja|倭文神社 (倉吉市)}} (Shizu, {{ill|Kurayoshi, Tottori|ja|倉吉市}}, [[Tottori Prefecture]])
Other notable Shidori Shrines include:
* '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tono)|ja|倭文神社 (遠野市)}}''' (Tsuchibuchi-cho, {{ill|Tono, Iwate|ja|遠野市}}, [[Iwate Prefecture]]) * '''{{ill|Shidori Shrine (Nara)|ja|倭文神社 (奈良市)}}''' (Nishikujo-cho, {{ill|Nara, Nara|ja|奈良市}}, [[Nara Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Kume District, Mimasaka Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Tsuyama)|ja|倭文神社 (津山市油木北)}} (Yukikita, {{ill|Tsuyama, Okayama|ja|津山市}}, [[Okayama Prefecture]]) * '''Shidori Shrine (倭文神社)''' in '''Shidori-go, Mihara District, Awaji Province''' – {{ill|Shidori Shrine (Awaji)|ja|倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)}} (Shidori, {{ill|Minamiawaji, Hyogo|ja|南あわじ市}}, [[Hyogo Prefecture]])
Additionally, '''{{ill|Oomiwa Shrine|ja|大甕神社}}''' ({{ill|Hitachi, Ibaraki|ja|日立市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) also enshrines '''Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto'''. Similarly, '''{{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社}}''' ({{ill|Kasama, Ibaraki|ja|笠間市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) enshrines the same deity, with a large sacred rock called "Mitarai" located to the south.
== Related Topics ==
* '''{{ill|Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto}}''' (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * '''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神}}''' (also known as Amanoorihime)
[[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Weaving and textiles in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to textile deities]] [[Category:Mythological and legendary Japanese figures]] [[ja:倭文神社]]
[[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines]] [[Category:倭文神社]][[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]]{{Shizuri Faith}}[[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines forks]]

''Shidori Shrine'' (倭文神社) refers to Shinto shrines bearing the name "Shidori" (also read as Shizuri, Shitori, or Shidori).
These shrines enshrine ''{{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto}}'' (also known as ''Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto'', ''Ameno Hachio'', or ''Takehatsuchi'') as the ''deity of weaving'', alongside ''{{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|ja|天棚機姫神}}'' (also known as ''Amano Yachichihime'' or ''Amanoorihime''). These shrines were established by the ''{{ill|Shidori clan|ja|倭文氏}}'', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The ''origin of these shrines is attributed to'' the ''{{ill|Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社}}'' in ''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to ''{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}'', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of ''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}''. Therefore, these shrines provide ''important historical evidence'' regarding ''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).''
Additionally, in the ''2016 animated film "Your Name"'', the fictional ''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社}}'' is depicted as enshrining ''Shidori deity Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto''.

[[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines]] [[Category:倭文神社]][[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]]{{Shizuri Faith}}[[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines forks]]

he ''{{ill|Shidori clan|ja|倭文氏}}'', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The ''origin of these shrines is attributed to'' the ''{{ill|Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|ja|葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社}}'' in ''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to ''{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}'', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of ''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}''. Therefore, these shrines provide ''important historical evidence'' regarding ''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).''
Additionally, in the ''2016 animated film "Your Name"'', the fictional ''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|ja|宮水神社}}'' is depicted as enshrining ''Shidori deity Takehatsuchi-no-Mikoto''.

[[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines]] [[Category:倭文神社]][[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]]{{Shizuri Faith]][[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines forks]]

he ''{{ill|Shidori clan|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:倭文氏]]'', who worshipped Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto as their ancestral deity.
The ''origin of these shrines is attributed to'' the ''{{ill|Katsuraki Shitori ni Imasu Ame no Ha Ikazuchi no Mikoto Shrine|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]]'' in ''Katsuragi, Nara Prefecture''. However, while the introduction and promotion of silk weaving technology in Japan is credited to ''{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:仁徳天皇]]'', some Shidori Shrines claim their origins date back to the reign of ''{{ill|Emperor Sujin|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:崇神天皇]]''. Therefore, these shrines provide ''important historical evidence'' regarding ''when and where silk weaving developed in Japan (Wa).''
Additionally, in the ''2016 animated film "Your Name"'', the fictional ''{{ill|Miyamizu Shrine|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:宮水神社]]'' is depicted as enshrining ''Shidori deity Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto''.

倭文{{ill|Muraji|ja|連}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} '''Shitori clan''' (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684年}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shitori Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - 倭文{{ill|Muraji|ja|連}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684年}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shitori Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - 倭文{{ill|Muraji|ja|連}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684年}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kinki region]] [[ja:倭文氏]] == Related Topics == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:天棚機姫神]] (also known as Amanoorihime) [[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Weaving and textiles in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to textile deities]] [[Category:Mythological and legendary Japanese figures]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines]] [[Category:倭文神社]][[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{Shizuri Faith}} [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines forks]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]]

倭文{{ill|Muraji|ja|連}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684年}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kinki region]] [[ja:倭文氏]] == Related Topics == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:天棚機姫神]] (also known as Amanoorihime) [[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Weaving and textiles in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to textile deities]] [[Category:Mythological and legendary Japanese figures]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines]] [[Category:倭文神社]][[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{Shizuri Faith}} [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines forks]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]]

Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kinki region]] [[ja:倭文氏]] == Related Topics == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:天棚機姫神]] (also known as Amanoorihime) [[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Weaving and textiles in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to textile deities]] [[Category:Mythological and legendary Japanese figures]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{Shizuri Faith}} [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines forks]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]]

Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684年}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kinki region]] [[ja:倭文氏]] == Related Topics == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:天棚機姫神]] (also known as Amanoorihime) [[Category:Shinto shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Weaving and textiles in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to textile deities]] [[Category:Mythological and legendary Japanese figures]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{Shizuri Faith}} [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines forks]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]]

[[ja:石井神社]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Shidori clan-qq]] [[ja:笠間市]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文氏}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684年}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684年}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kinki region]] [[ja:倭文氏]] == Related Topics == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Shidori clan-qq]] [[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Shidori clan-qq]] [[ja:天棚機姫神]] (also known as Amanoorihime) [[Category:Shinto shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Weaving and textiles in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to textile deities]] [[Category:Mythological and legendary Japanese figures]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} {{Shizuri Faith}} [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines forks]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] }}[[Category:Shidori clan}}

According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[ja:笠間市]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文氏}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684年}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kinki region]] [[ja:倭文氏]] == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[ja:天棚機姫神]] (also known as Amanoorihime) [[Category:Shinto shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Weaving and textiles in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to textile deities]] [[Category:Mythological and legendary Japanese figures]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} {{Shizuri Faith}} [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines forks]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] }}[[Category:Shidori clan}}

Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684年}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kinki region]] [[ja:倭文氏]] == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:New pages]] [[ja:天棚機姫神]] (also known as Amanoorihime) [[Category:Shinto shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Weaving and textiles in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to textile deities]] [[Category:Mythological and legendary Japanese figures]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} {{Shizuri Faith}} [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines forks]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] }}[[Category:Shidori clan}}

[[Category:Unsorted_completion_categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[ja:笠間市]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文氏}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684年}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kinki region]] [[ja:倭文氏]] == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:Unsorted_completion_categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:Unsorted_completion_categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[ja:天棚機姫神]] (also known as Amanoorihime) [[Category:Shinto shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Weaving and textiles in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to textile deities]] [[Category:Mythological and legendary Japanese figures]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} {{Shizuri Faith}} [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines forks]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] }}[[Category:Shidori clan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Shidori clan}}

[[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[ja:笠間市]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文氏}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684年}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684年}}). According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kinki region]] [[ja:倭文氏]] == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[ja:天棚機姫神]] (also known as Amanoorihime) [[Category:Shinto shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Weaving and textiles in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to textile deities]] [[Category:Mythological and legendary Japanese figures]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} {{Shizuri Faith}} [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines forks]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] }}[[Category:Shidori clan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Shidori clan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Shidori clan}}

[[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:笠間市]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文氏}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684年}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kinki region]] [[ja:倭文氏]] == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:天棚機姫神]] (also known as Amanoorihime) [[Category:Shinto shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Weaving and textiles in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to textile deities]] [[Category:Mythological and legendary Japanese figures]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} {{Shizuri Faith}} [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines forks]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:qq]] }}[[Category:Shidori clan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Shidori clan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Shidori clan}}
