{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684年}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:笠間市]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文氏}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684年}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kinki region]] [[ja:倭文氏]] == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:天棚機姫神]] (also known as Amanoorihime) [[Category:Shinto shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Weaving and textiles in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to textile deities]] [[Category:Mythological and legendary Japanese figures]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} {{Shizuri Faith}} [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines forks]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:qq]] }}[[Category:Shidori clan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Shidori clan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Shidori clan}}

{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684年}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:笠間市]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文氏}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684年}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Clans of the Kinki region]] [[ja:倭文氏]] == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:天棚機姫神]] (also known as Amanoorihime) [[Category:Shinto shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Weaving and textiles in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to textile deities]] [[Category:Mythological and legendary Japanese figures]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Imported Shinto cults]] [[Category:kk]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} {{Shizuri Faith}} [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] [[Category:Shizuri Shrines forks]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:qq]] }}[[Category:Shidori clan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Shidori clan}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Shidori clan}} [[Category:New]]

Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰}} (Yamato)

An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684年}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - clans]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:Important original categories]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[ja:笠間市]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文氏}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684年}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * clans]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:Important original categories]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[ja:笠間市]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文氏}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684年}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Important original categories]][[Category:japanese clans]] |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684年}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - [[ja:石井神社]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[ja:笠間市]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文氏}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684年}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[ja:倭文氏]]== See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[ja:天棚機姫神]] (also known as Amanoorihime) [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} {{Shizuri Faith}} [[Category:japanese clans]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }}[[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]]

[[ja:石井神社]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[ja:笠間市]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文氏}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684年}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[ja:倭文氏]]== See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[ja:天棚機姫神]] (also known as Amanoorihime) [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} {{Shizuri Faith}} [[Category:japanese clans]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }}[[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:return the see also]]

Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684年}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[ja:倭文氏]] == See Also! [[ja:石井神社]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[ja:笠間市]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文氏}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684年}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684th year }}). According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815年}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - [[ja:石井神社]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[ja:笠間市]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文氏}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684th year }}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815th year }}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - {{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684th year }}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815th year }}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[ja:倭文氏]] == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}}[[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[ja:天棚機姫神]] (also known as Amanoorihime) [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]][[ja:倭文神社]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} {{Shizuri Faith}} [[Category:japanese clans]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:japanese clans]][[sn:Shidori clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }}[[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]][[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:return the see also]]

An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684th year }}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815th year }}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815th year }}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en}} (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}} (also known as Amanoorihime) {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} }} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Shidori clan]] [[ja:倭文氏]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[ja:仁徳天皇]] [[ja:崇神天皇]] [[ja:宮水神社]] [[ja:延喜式神名帳]] [[ja:倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)]] [[ja:伊勢崎市]] [[ja:静神社]] [[ja:那珂市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (韮崎市)]] [[ja:韮崎市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:富士宮市]] [[ja:鍬戸神社]] [[ja:三島市]] [[ja:加佐登神社]] [[ja:倭文神社 (舞鶴市)]] [[ja:舞鶴市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (与謝野町)]] [[ja:与謝野町]] [[ja:倭文神社 (朝来市)]] [[ja:朝来市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (鳥取市)]] [[ja:鳥取市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (湯梨浜町)]] [[ja:湯梨浜町]] [[ja:倭文神社 (倉吉市)]] [[ja:倉吉市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (遠野市)]] [[ja:遠野市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]] [[ja:奈良市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (津山市油木北)]] [[ja:津山市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)]] [[ja:南あわじ市]] [[ja:大甕神社]] [[ja:日立市]] [[ja:石井神社]] [[ja:笠間市]] [[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:天棚機姫神]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]]

{{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文氏|12=draft}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|12=draft}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|12=draft}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|12=draft}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰|12=draft}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命|12=draft}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別|12=draft}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods|12=draft}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|12=draft}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏|12=draft}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=draft}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|12=draft}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制|12=draft}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|12=draft}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ|12=draft}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ|12=draft}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=draft}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|12=draft}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|12=draft}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684th year|12=draft}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|12=draft}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首|12=draft}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815th year|12=draft}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|12=draft}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|12=draft}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|12=draft}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|12=draft}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=draft}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|12=draft}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines|12=draft}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清|12=draft}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=draft}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭|12=draft}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎|12=draft}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元|12=draft}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房|12=draft}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en|12=draft}} (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}} (also known as Amanoorihime) {{citation needed|Shidori clan|12=draft}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} }} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Shidori clan]] [[ja:倭文氏]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[ja:仁徳天皇]] [[ja:崇神天皇]] [[ja:宮水神社]] [[ja:延喜式神名帳]] [[ja:倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)]] [[ja:伊勢崎市]] [[ja:静神社]] [[ja:那珂市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (韮崎市)]] [[ja:韮崎市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:富士宮市]] [[ja:鍬戸神社]] [[ja:三島市]] [[ja:加佐登神社]] [[ja:倭文神社 (舞鶴市)]] [[ja:舞鶴市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (与謝野町)]] [[ja:与謝野町]] [[ja:倭文神社 (朝来市)]] [[ja:朝来市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (鳥取市)]] [[ja:鳥取市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (湯梨浜町)]] [[ja:湯梨浜町]] [[ja:倭文神社 (倉吉市)]] [[ja:倉吉市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (遠野市)]] [[ja:遠野市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]] [[ja:奈良市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (津山市油木北)]] [[ja:津山市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)]] [[ja:南あわじ市]] [[ja:大甕神社]] [[ja:日立市]] [[ja:石井神社]] [[ja:笠間市]] [[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:天棚機姫神]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]]

Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|12=draft}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|12=draft}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰|12=draft}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命|12=draft}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別|12=draft}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods|12=draft}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|12=draft}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏|12=draft}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=draft}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|12=draft}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制|12=draft}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|12=draft}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ|12=draft}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ|12=draft}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=draft}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|12=draft}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|12=draft}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684th year|12=draft}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|12=draft}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首|12=draft}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815th year|12=draft}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|12=draft}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|12=draft}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|12=draft}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|12=draft}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=draft}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|12=draft}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines|12=draft}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清|12=draft}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=draft}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭|12=draft}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎|12=draft}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元|12=draft}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房|12=draft}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en|12=draft}} (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}} (also known as Amanoorihime) {{citation needed|Shidori clan|12=draft}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} }} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Shidori clan]] [[ja:倭文氏]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[ja:仁徳天皇]] [[ja:崇神天皇]] [[ja:宮水神社]] [[ja:延喜式神名帳]] [[ja:倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)]] [[ja:伊勢崎市]] [[ja:静神社]] [[ja:那珂市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (韮崎市)]] [[ja:韮崎市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:富士宮市]] [[ja:鍬戸神社]] [[ja:三島市]] [[ja:加佐登神社]] [[ja:倭文神社 (舞鶴市)]] [[ja:舞鶴市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (与謝野町)]] [[ja:与謝野町]] [[ja:倭文神社 (朝来市)]] [[ja:朝来市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (鳥取市)]] [[ja:鳥取市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (湯梨浜町)]] [[ja:湯梨浜町]] [[ja:倭文神社 (倉吉市)]] [[ja:倉吉市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (遠野市)]] [[ja:遠野市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]] [[ja:奈良市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (津山市油木北)]] [[ja:津山市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)]] [[ja:南あわじ市]] [[ja:大甕神社]] [[ja:日立市]] [[ja:石井神社]] [[ja:笠間市]] [[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:天棚機姫神]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]]
