revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文氏|12=draft}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|12=draft}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|12=draft}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|12=draft}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰|12=draft}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命|12=draft}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別|12=draft}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods|12=draft}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|12=draft}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏|12=draft}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=draft}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|12=draft}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制|12=draft}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|12=draft}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ|12=draft}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ|12=draft}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=draft}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|12=draft}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|12=draft}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684th year|12=draft}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|12=draft}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首|12=draft}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815th year|12=draft}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|12=draft}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|12=draft}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|12=draft}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|12=draft}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=draft}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|12=draft}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines|12=draft}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清|12=draft}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=draft}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭|12=draft}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎|12=draft}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元|12=draft}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房|12=draft}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en|12=draft}} (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}} (also known as Amanoorihime) {{citation needed|Shidori clan|12=draft}} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} }} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shidori clan]] [[ja:倭文氏]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[ja:仁徳天皇]] [[ja:崇神天皇]] [[ja:宮水神社]] [[ja:延喜式神名帳]] [[ja:倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)]] [[ja:伊勢崎市]] [[ja:静神社]] [[ja:那珂市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (韮崎市)]] [[ja:韮崎市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:富士宮市]] [[ja:鍬戸神社]] [[ja:三島市]] [[ja:加佐登神社]] [[ja:倭文神社 (舞鶴市)]] [[ja:舞鶴市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (与謝野町)]] [[ja:与謝野町]] [[ja:倭文神社 (朝来市)]] [[ja:朝来市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (鳥取市)]] [[ja:鳥取市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (湯梨浜町)]] [[ja:湯梨浜町]] [[ja:倭文神社 (倉吉市)]] [[ja:倉吉市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (遠野市)]] [[ja:遠野市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]] [[ja:奈良市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (津山市油木北)]] [[ja:津山市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)]] [[ja:南あわじ市]] [[ja:大甕神社]] [[ja:日立市]] [[ja:石井神社]] [[ja:笠間市]] [[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:天棚機姫神]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]]

An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=draft}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|12=draft}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制|12=draft}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|12=draft}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ|12=draft}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ|12=draft}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=draft}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|12=draft}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|12=draft}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684th year|12=draft}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|12=draft}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首|12=draft}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815th year|12=draft}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|12=draft}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|12=draft}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|12=draft}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|12=draft}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=draft}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|12=draft}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines|12=draft}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清|12=draft}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=draft}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭|12=draft}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎|12=draft}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元|12=draft}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房|12=draft}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en|12=draft}} (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}} (also known as Amanoorihime) {{citation needed|Shidori clan|12=draft}} [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} }} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] }} [[sn:Shidori clan]] [[ja:倭文氏]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[ja:仁徳天皇]] [[ja:崇神天皇]] [[ja:宮水神社]] [[ja:延喜式神名帳]] [[ja:倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)]] [[ja:伊勢崎市]] [[ja:静神社]] [[ja:那珂市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (韮崎市)]] [[ja:韮崎市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:富士宮市]] [[ja:鍬戸神社]] [[ja:三島市]] [[ja:加佐登神社]] [[ja:倭文神社 (舞鶴市)]] [[ja:舞鶴市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (与謝野町)]] [[ja:与謝野町]] [[ja:倭文神社 (朝来市)]] [[ja:朝来市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (鳥取市)]] [[ja:鳥取市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (湯梨浜町)]] [[ja:湯梨浜町]] [[ja:倭文神社 (倉吉市)]] [[ja:倉吉市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (遠野市)]] [[ja:遠野市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]] [[ja:奈良市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (津山市油木北)]] [[ja:津山市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)]] [[ja:南あわじ市]] [[ja:大甕神社]] [[ja:日立市]] [[ja:石井神社]] [[ja:笠間市]] [[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:天棚機姫神]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]]

Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en|12=draft}} (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}} (also known as Amanoorihime) {{citation needed|Shidori clan|12=draft}} [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} }} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] }} [[sn:Shidori clan]] [[ja:倭文氏]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[ja:仁徳天皇]] [[ja:崇神天皇]] [[ja:宮水神社]] [[ja:延喜式神名帳]] [[ja:倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)]] [[ja:伊勢崎市]] [[ja:静神社]] [[ja:那珂市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (韮崎市)]] [[ja:韮崎市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:富士宮市]] [[ja:鍬戸神社]] [[ja:三島市]] [[ja:加佐登神社]] [[ja:倭文神社 (舞鶴市)]] [[ja:舞鶴市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (与謝野町)]] [[ja:与謝野町]] [[ja:倭文神社 (朝来市)]] [[ja:朝来市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (鳥取市)]] [[ja:鳥取市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (湯梨浜町)]] [[ja:湯梨浜町]] [[ja:倭文神社 (倉吉市)]] [[ja:倉吉市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (遠野市)]] [[ja:遠野市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]] [[ja:奈良市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (津山市油木北)]] [[ja:津山市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)]] [[ja:南あわじ市]] [[ja:大甕神社]] [[ja:日立市]] [[ja:石井神社]] [[ja:笠間市]] [[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:天棚機姫神]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] {{Shizuri Faith}}

{{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文氏|12=draft}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|12=draft}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|12=draft}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=draft}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰|12=draft}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命|12=draft}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別|12=draft}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods|12=draft}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|12=draft}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏|12=draft}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=draft}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|12=draft}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制|12=draft}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|12=draft}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ|12=draft}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ|12=draft}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=draft}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|12=draft}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|12=draft}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684th year|12=draft}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|12=draft}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首|12=draft}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815th year|12=draft}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|12=draft}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|12=draft}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|12=draft}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|12=draft}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=draft}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=draft}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines|12=draft}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清|12=draft}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=draft}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭|12=draft}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎|12=draft}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元|12=draft}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房|12=draft}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en|12=draft}} (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}} (also known as Amanoorihime) {{citation needed|Shidori clan|12=draft}} [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} }} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] }} [[sn:Shidori clan]] [[ja:倭文氏]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[ja:仁徳天皇]] [[ja:崇神天皇]] [[ja:宮水神社]] [[ja:延喜式神名帳]] [[ja:倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)]] [[ja:伊勢崎市]] [[ja:静神社]] [[ja:那珂市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (韮崎市)]] [[ja:韮崎市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:富士宮市]] [[ja:鍬戸神社]] [[ja:三島市]] [[ja:加佐登神社]] [[ja:倭文神社 (舞鶴市)]] [[ja:舞鶴市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (与謝野町)]] [[ja:与謝野町]] [[ja:倭文神社 (朝来市)]] [[ja:朝来市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (鳥取市)]] [[ja:鳥取市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (湯梨浜町)]] [[ja:湯梨浜町]] [[ja:倭文神社 (倉吉市)]] [[ja:倉吉市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (遠野市)]] [[ja:遠野市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]] [[ja:奈良市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (津山市油木北)]] [[ja:津山市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)]] [[ja:南あわじ市]] [[ja:大甕神社]] [[ja:日立市]] [[ja:石井神社]] [[ja:笠間市]] [[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:天棚機姫神]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] {{Shizuri Faith}}

Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=draft}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=draft}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines|12=draft}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清|12=draft}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=draft}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭|12=draft}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎|12=draft}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元|12=draft}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房|12=draft}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en|12=draft}} (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}} (also known as Amanoorihime) {{citation needed|Shidori clan|12=draft}} [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} }} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] }} [[sn:Shidori clan]] [[ja:倭文氏]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[ja:仁徳天皇]] [[ja:崇神天皇]] [[ja:宮水神社]] [[ja:延喜式神名帳]] [[ja:倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)]] [[ja:伊勢崎市]] [[ja:静神社]] [[ja:那珂市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (韮崎市)]] [[ja:韮崎市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:富士宮市]] [[ja:鍬戸神社]] [[ja:三島市]] [[ja:加佐登神社]] [[ja:倭文神社 (舞鶴市)]] [[ja:舞鶴市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (与謝野町)]] [[ja:与謝野町]] [[ja:倭文神社 (朝来市)]] [[ja:朝来市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (鳥取市)]] [[ja:鳥取市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (湯梨浜町)]] [[ja:湯梨浜町]] [[ja:倭文神社 (倉吉市)]] [[ja:倉吉市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (遠野市)]] [[ja:遠野市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]] [[ja:奈良市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (津山市油木北)]] [[ja:津山市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)]] [[ja:南あわじ市]] [[ja:大甕神社]] [[ja:日立市]] [[ja:石井神社]] [[ja:笠間市]] [[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:天棚機姫神]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] {{Shizuri Faith}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|倭文氏|" to "|倭文部|1=Shidori clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shidori clan|"
revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shidori clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shidori clan|12=draft}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|12=draft}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|12=draft}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=draft}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰|12=draft}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命|12=draft}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別|12=draft}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods|12=draft}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|12=draft}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|12=draft}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏|12=draft}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=draft}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|1=Shidori clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|12=draft}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制|12=draft}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|12=draft}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ|12=draft}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ|12=draft}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=draft}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|12=draft}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|12=draft}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684th year|12=draft}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|12=draft}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首|12=draft}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815th year|12=draft}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|12=draft}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|12=draft}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|12=draft}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|12=draft}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=draft}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=draft}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines|12=draft}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清|12=draft}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=draft}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭|12=draft}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎|12=draft}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元|12=draft}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房|12=draft}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en|12=draft}} (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}} (also known as Amanoorihime) {{citation needed|Shidori clan|12=draft}} [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Shidori clan}} }} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] }} [[sn:Shidori clan]] [[ja:倭文氏]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[ja:仁徳天皇]] [[ja:崇神天皇]] [[ja:宮水神社]] [[ja:延喜式神名帳]] [[ja:倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)]] [[ja:伊勢崎市]] [[ja:静神社]] [[ja:那珂市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (韮崎市)]] [[ja:韮崎市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:富士宮市]] [[ja:鍬戸神社]] [[ja:三島市]] [[ja:加佐登神社]] [[ja:倭文神社 (舞鶴市)]] [[ja:舞鶴市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (与謝野町)]] [[ja:与謝野町]] [[ja:倭文神社 (朝来市)]] [[ja:朝来市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (鳥取市)]] [[ja:鳥取市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (湯梨浜町)]] [[ja:湯梨浜町]] [[ja:倭文神社 (倉吉市)]] [[ja:倉吉市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (遠野市)]] [[ja:遠野市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]] [[ja:奈良市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (津山市油木北)]] [[ja:津山市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)]] [[ja:南あわじ市]] [[ja:大甕神社]] [[ja:日立市]] [[ja:石井神社]] [[ja:笠間市]] [[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:天棚機姫神]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] {{Shizuri Faith}}

Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=draft}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=draft}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines|12=draft}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清|12=draft}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=draft}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭|12=draft}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎|12=draft}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元|12=draft}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房|12=draft}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en|12=draft}} (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}} (also known as Amanoorihime) {{citation needed|Shizuri clan|12=draft}} [[Category:Shizuri Faith]] {{citation needed|Shizuri clan}} }} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] }} [[sn:Shizuri clan]] [[ja:倭文氏]] [[ja:葛木倭文座天羽雷命神社]] [[ja:仁徳天皇]] [[ja:崇神天皇]] [[ja:宮水神社]] [[ja:延喜式神名帳]] [[ja:倭文神社 (伊勢崎市)]] [[ja:伊勢崎市]] [[ja:静神社]] [[ja:那珂市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (韮崎市)]] [[ja:韮崎市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:富士宮市]] [[ja:鍬戸神社]] [[ja:三島市]] [[ja:加佐登神社]] [[ja:倭文神社 (舞鶴市)]] [[ja:舞鶴市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (与謝野町)]] [[ja:与謝野町]] [[ja:倭文神社 (朝来市)]] [[ja:朝来市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (鳥取市)]] [[ja:鳥取市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (湯梨浜町)]] [[ja:湯梨浜町]] [[ja:倭文神社 (倉吉市)]] [[ja:倉吉市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (遠野市)]] [[ja:遠野市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (奈良市)]] [[ja:奈良市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (津山市油木北)]] [[ja:津山市]] [[ja:倭文神社 (あわじ市倭文委文)]] [[ja:南あわじ市]] [[ja:大甕神社]] [[ja:日立市]] [[ja:石井神社]] [[ja:笠間市]] [[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:天棚機姫神]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]] {{Shizuri Faith}}

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=draft|12=draft}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|12=draft|12=draft}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=draft|12=draft}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods|12=draft|12=draft}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|12=draft|12=draft}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=draft|12=draft}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|12=draft|12=draft}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物|12=draft|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|12=draft|12=draft}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|12=draft|12=draft}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|12=draft|12=draft}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684th year|12=draft|12=draft}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首|12=draft|12=draft}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815th year|12=draft|12=draft}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|12=draft|12=draft}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|12=draft|12=draft}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|12=draft|12=draft}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|12=draft|12=draft}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=draft|12=draft}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=draft|12=draft}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines|12=draft|12=draft}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清|12=draft|12=draft}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭|12=draft|12=draft}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元|12=draft|12=draft}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=draft|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝|12=draft|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-|12=draft|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World|12=draft|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=draft|12=draft}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記|12=draft|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}} (also known as Amanoorihime) {{citation needed|Shizuri clan|12=draft|12=draft}} {{citation needed|Shizuri clan}} }} revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=draft|12=draft}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|12=draft|12=draft}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=draft|12=draft}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods|12=draft|12=draft}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|12=draft|12=draft}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏|12=draft|12=draft}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=draft|12=draft}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|12=draft|12=draft}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物|12=draft|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|12=draft|12=draft}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|12=draft|12=draft}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|12=draft|12=draft}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684th year|12=draft|12=draft}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首|12=draft|12=draft}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815th year|12=draft|12=draft}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|12=draft|12=draft}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|12=draft|12=draft}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|12=draft|12=draft}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|12=draft|12=draft}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=draft|12=draft}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=draft|12=draft}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines|12=draft|12=draft}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清|12=draft|12=draft}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭|12=draft|12=draft}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元|12=draft|12=draft}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記|12=draft|12=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} == See Also == * (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}} (also known as Amanoorihime) {{citation needed|Shizuri clan|12=draft|12=draft}} {{citation needed|Shizuri clan}} }} {{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏|1=Uji (clan)|lt=Uji|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏|1=Uji (clan)|lt=Uji|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} == See Also == * (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en}} (also known as Amanoorihime) {{citation needed|Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{citation needed|Shizuri clan}} }} revision.comment: 5 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:倭文氏 up to rev 97888519
revision.text:
{{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = 倭文氏 |画像 = |氏姓 = 倭文[[宿禰]]（大和・河内）
倭文[[連]]（摂津） |始祖 = [[天羽槌雄神]] |出自 = |氏祖 = [[大味宿禰]]（大和）
[[天伊佐布魂命]]（摂津） |種別 = [[神別]]（[[天津神・国津神|天神]]） |本貫 = [[大和国]]、[[河内国]]、[[摂津国]]ほか |人物 = |後裔 = }} '''倭文氏'''（しとりうじ、しどりうじ、しずりうじ）は、「倭文」を[[氏#古代氏族としての「氏」|氏]]の名とする[[氏族]]。異表記は「委文」。 == 概要 == [[織物]]を生産する[[部民制|部民]]である倭文部（しとりべ）を率いた[[伴造]]氏族[[#ujizoku|日本古代氏族事典]]－「しとり［委文］」。倭文とはシズオリの意で、[[アサ]]や[[カジノキ]]などの繊維で文様を織り出した日本古来の織物[[#kodaishi|日本古代史大辞典]]－「しどり［倭文］」。 中央の一族は[[連]]の姓（[[カバネ]]）であり、その中の主流の一族は[[天武天皇]]13年（[[684年]]）に[[宿禰]]姓を賜った。地方の伴造には、[[連]]、[[臣]]、[[首 (姓)|首]]などの姓がある。 『[[新撰姓氏録]]』（[[815年]]）によると、[[大和国]]と[[河内国]]に委文宿禰、[[摂津国]]に委文連の三氏を掲げている。出自としては、「大和国[[神別]]（[[天津神・国津神|天神]]）」の項に「委文宿禰　出自[[カミムスビ|神魂命]]之後[[大味宿禰]]也」とあり、また「摂津国神別（天神）」の項に「委文連　[[角凝魂命]]男[[伊佐布魂命]]之後也」とある[[#izumo|倭文氏と下照姫　倭文神社（２）]]。 [[織機|機織]]の神である[[天羽槌雄神]]を[[祖神]]として奉斎し、全国に[[倭文神社]]が残る。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Notelist}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{citation|和書|editor=[[佐伯有清]]|title=日本古代氏族事典【新装版】|publisher=[[雄山閣]]|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|和書|editor=上田正昭：監／井上満郎・愛宕元：|title=日本古代史大辞典|publisher=大和書房|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title=倭文氏と下照姫　倭文神社（２）|work=蘇える出雲王朝|accessdate=2020-01-22|ref=izumo}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title=社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-|work=家紋World|accessdate=2020-01-22|ref=shake}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title=天羽槌雄神|work=玄松子の祭神記|accessdate=2020-01-22|ref=izumo}} == 関連項目 == * [[倭文神社]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しとりうし}} [[Category:氏]] [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[Category:近畿地方の氏族]]

{{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = 倭文氏 |画像 = |氏姓 = 倭文[[宿禰]]（大和・河内）
倭文[[連]]（摂津） |始祖 = [[天羽槌雄神]] |出自 = |氏祖 = [[大味宿禰]]（大和）

{{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏|1=Uji (clan)|lt=Uji|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * [[ja:天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:天棚機姫神]] [[ja:倭文神社]] [[ja:倭文神社|しすりしんしや]]

{{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏|1=Uji (clan)|lt=Uji|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} == See Also == * revision.comment: Bot: add simplewiki draft link to ill templates
revision.text:
{{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukune|qq=}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraji|qq=}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōaji no Sukune|qq=}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|qq=}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly and Earthly Gods|qq=}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Province|qq=}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Province|qq=}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏|1=Uji (clan)|lt=Uji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/uji|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji (clan)|qq=}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|qq=}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitoribe|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/textiles|qq=}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Be system|qq=}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|qq=}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hemp|qq=}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paper mulberry|qq=}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|qq=}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraji|qq=}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukune|qq=}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/684 AD|qq=}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraji|qq=}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashira|qq=}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/815 AD|qq=}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Province|qq=}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Province|qq=}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusuhi|qq=}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunogoromusuhi|qq=}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|qq=}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Arikiyo|qq=}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzankaku|qq=}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Masahiro|qq=}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Mitsuo|qq=}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Gen|qq=}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Shobo|qq=}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|qq=}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|qq=}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|qq=}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family Crest World|qq=}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|qq=}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} == See Also == * (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Amanoorihime) }} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated {{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukune|qq=}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraji|qq=}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōaji no Sukune|qq=}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|qq=}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Heavenly Gods|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly and Earthly Gods|qq=}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Province|qq=}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Province|qq=}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|uji|ja|氏|1=Uji (clan)|lt=Uji|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/uji|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji (clan)|qq=}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|qq=}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitoribe|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|textiles|ja|織物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/textiles|qq=}} under the {{ill|Be system|ja|部民制|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Be system|qq=}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|qq=}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|hemp|ja|アサ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hemp|qq=}} and {{ill|paper mulberry|ja|カジノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/paper mulberry|qq=}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|qq=}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraji|qq=}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukune|qq=}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684 AD|ja|684th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/684 AD|qq=}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|ja|連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraji|qq=}}, {{ill|Omi|ja|臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kashira|ja|首|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashira|qq=}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}} (compiled in {{ill|815 AD|ja|815th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/815 AD|qq=}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Province|qq=}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|ja|摂津国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Settsu Province|qq=}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusuhi|qq=}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunogoromusuhi|qq=}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|qq=}} The Shitori clan enshrined
