An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitoribe|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, a group responsible for producing [[:en:Textile|textiles]] under the [[:en:Bemin|Be system]].{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as [[:en:Cannabis|hemp]] and [[:en:Paper mulberry|paper mulberry]].{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title [[:en:Muraji|Muraji]], and its main line was granted the title [[:en:Sukune|Sukune]] in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ([[:en:684|684 AD]]). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as [[:en:Muraji|Muraji]], [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], and [[:en:Neck|Kashira]]. According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] (compiled in [[:en:815|815 AD]]), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and *Itabumi no Muraji* in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunogoromusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} remain across [[ja:倭文氏]] [[ja:倭文氏]] [[ja:倭文氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85876607]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no [[:en:Sukune|Sukune]] (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no [[:en:Muraji|Muraji]] (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōaji no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神|6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|lt=Heavenly Gods|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly and Earthly Gods|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) |main_location = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[:en:Clan|Clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its [[:en:Uji (clan)|Uji]]. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitoribe|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, a group responsible for producing [[:en:Textile|textiles]] under the [[:en:Bemin|Be system]].{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as [[:en:Cannabis|hemp]] and [[:en:Paper mulberry|paper mulberry]].{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title [[:en:Muraji|Muraji]], and its main line was granted the title [[:en:Sukune|Sukune]] in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ([[:en:684|684 AD]]). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as [[:en:Muraji|Muraji]], [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], and [[:en:Neck|Kashira]]. According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] (compiled in [[:en:815|815 AD]]), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and *Itabumi no Muraji* in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunogoromusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Arikiyo|1=Arikiyo Saeki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13364959}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzankaku|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Masahiro|1=Masaaki Ueda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3297094}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Mitsuo|1=Mitsuo Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16264281}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Gen|1=Hajime Otagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493824}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Shobo|1=Daiwa Shobō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433473}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family Crest World|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|1=Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|zh|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi-no-Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahatsuchi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} (also known as Amenohaikazuchi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanabatabime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as Amanoorihime) }} {{Shizuri Faith}} {{translated An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitoribe|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, a group responsible for producing [[:en:Textile|textiles]] under the [[:en:Bemin|Be system]].{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as [[:en:Cannabis|hemp]] and [[:en:Paper mulberry|paper mulberry]].{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title [[:en:Muraji|Muraji]], and its main line was granted the title [[:en:Sukune|Sukune]] in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ([[:en:684|684 AD]]). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as [[:en:Muraji|Muraji]], [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], and [[:en:Neck|Kashira]]. According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] (compiled in [[:en:815|815 AD]]), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and *Itabumi no Muraji* in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunogoromusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}, [[ja:倭文氏]] [[ja:倭文氏]] [[ja:倭文氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85876607]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no [[:en:Sukune|Sukune]] (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no [[:en:Muraji|Muraji]] (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōaji no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神|6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|lt=Heavenly Gods|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly and Earthly Gods|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) |main_location = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[:en:Clan|Clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its [[:en:Uji (clan)|Uji]]. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitoribe|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, a group responsible for producing [[:en:Textile|textiles]] under the [[:en:Bemin|Be system]].{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as [[:en:Cannabis|hemp]] and [[:en:Paper mulberry|paper mulberry]].{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title [[:en:Muraji|Muraji]], and its main line was granted the title [[:en:Sukune|Sukune]] in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ([[:en:684|684 AD]]). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as [[:en:Muraji|Muraji]], [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], and [[:en:Neck|Kashira]]. According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] (compiled in [[:en:815|815 AD]]), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and *Itabumi no Muraji* in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunogoromusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} remain across [[ja:倭文氏]] [[ja:倭文氏]] [[ja:倭文氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85876607]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no [[:en:Sukune|Sukune]] (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no [[:en:Muraji|Muraji]] (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōaji no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神|6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|lt=Heavenly Gods|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly and Earthly Gods|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) |main_location = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[:en:Clan|Clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its [[:en:Uji (clan)|Uji]]. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitoribe|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, a group responsible for producing [[:en:Textile|textiles]] under the [[:en:Bemin|Be system]].{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as [[:en:Cannabis|hemp]] and [[:en:Paper mulberry|paper mulberry]].{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title [[:en:Muraji|Muraji]], and its main line was granted the title [[:en:Sukune|Sukune]] in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ([[:en:684|684 AD]]). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as [[:en:Muraji|Muraji]], [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], and [[:en:Neck|Kashira]]. According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] (compiled in [[:en:815|815 AD]]), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and *Itabumi no Muraji* in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunogoromusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Arikiyo|1=Arikiyo Saeki|comment=changed title based on {{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神|6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|lt=Heavenly Gods|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly and Earthly Gods|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) |main_location = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[:en:Clan|Clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its [[:en:Uji (clan)|Uji]]. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitoribe|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, a group responsible for producing [[:en:Textile|textiles]] under the [[:en:Bemin|Be system]].{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as [[:en:Cannabis|hemp]] and [[:en:Paper mulberry|paper mulberry]].{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title [[:en:Muraji|Muraji]], and its main line was granted the title [[:en:Sukune|Sukune]] in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ([[:en:684|684 AD]]). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as [[:en:Muraji|Muraji]], [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], and [[:en:Neck|Kashira]]. According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] (compiled in [[:en:815|815 AD]]), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and *Itabumi no Muraji* in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunogoromusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} remain across According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] (compiled in [[:en:815|815 AD]]), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and *Itabumi no Muraji* in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." [[ja:倭文氏]] [[ja:倭文氏]] [[ja:倭文氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85876607]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no [[:en:Sukune|Sukune]] (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no [[:en:Muraji|Muraji]] (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōaji no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神|6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|lt=Heavenly Gods|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly and Earthly Gods|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) |main_location = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]], and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a [[:en:Clan|Clan]] that bore the name "倭文" as its [[:en:Uji (clan)|Uji]]. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitoribe|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, a group responsible for producing [[:en:Textile|textiles]] under the [[:en:Bemin|Be system]].{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as [[:en:Cannabis|hemp]] and [[:en:Paper mulberry|paper mulberry]].{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title [[:en:Muraji|Muraji]], and its main line was granted the title [[:en:Sukune|Sukune]] in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ([[:en:684|684 AD]]). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as [[:en:Muraji|Muraji]], [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], and [[:en:Neck|Kashira]]. According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] (compiled in [[:en:815|815 AD]]), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and *Itabumi no Muraji* in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunogoromusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Arikiyo|1=Arikiyo Saeki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13364959}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzankaku|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Masahiro|1=Masaaki Ueda|comment=changed title based on Gen|ja|愛宕元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Gen|1=Hajime Otagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493824}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Shobo|1=Daiwa Shobō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433473}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as [[:en:Muraji|Muraji]], [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], and [[:en:Neck|Kashira]]. According to the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]] (compiled in [[:en:815|815 AD]]), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]], and *Itabumi no Muraji* in [[:en:Settsu Province|Settsu Province]]. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunogoromusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
[[ja:倭文氏]] [[ja:倭文氏]] [[ja:倭文氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85876607]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|en|Sukune|WD=Q11455653|lt=Sukune}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōaji no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神|6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|lt=Heavenly Gods|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly and Earthly Gods|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=Clan}} that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|Uji (clan)|en|Uji (clan)|WD=Q2575910|lt=Uji}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitoribe|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|Textile|en|Textile|WD=Q28823|lt=textiles}} under the {{ill|Bemin|en|Bemin|WD=Q2892644|lt=Be system}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|Cannabis|en|Cannabis|WD=Q79817|lt=hemp}} and {{ill|Paper mulberry|en|Paper mulberry|WD=Q389185|lt=paper mulberry}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|en|Sukune|WD=Q11455653|lt=Sukune}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684|en|684|WD=Q31128|lt=684 AD}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji}}, {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Omi}}, and {{ill|Neck|en|Neck|WD=Q9633|lt=Kashira}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} (compiled in {{ill|815|en|815|WD=Q29917|lt=815 AD}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunogoromusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Arikiyo|1=Arikiyo Saeki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13364959}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzankaku|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Masahiro|1=Masaaki Ueda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3297094}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Mitsuo|1=Mitsuo Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16264281}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Gen|1=Hajime Otagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493824}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Shobo|1=Daiwa Shobō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433473}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family Crest World|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōaji no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神|6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|lt=Heavenly Gods|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly and Earthly Gods|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=Clan}} that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|Uji (clan)|en|Uji (clan)|WD=Q2575910|lt=Uji}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitoribe|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|Textile|en|Textile|WD=Q28823|lt=textiles}} under the {{ill|Bemin|en|Bemin|WD=Q2892644|lt=Be system}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|Cannabis|en|Cannabis|WD=Q79817|lt=hemp}} and {{ill|Paper mulberry|en|Paper mulberry|WD=Q389185|lt=paper mulberry}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|en|Sukune|WD=Q11455653|lt=Sukune}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684|en|684|WD=Q31128|lt=684 AD}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji}}, {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Omi}}, and {{ill|Neck|en|Neck|WD=Q9633|lt=Kashira}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} (compiled in {{ill|815|en|815|WD=Q29917|lt=815 AD}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunogoromusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} remain across An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitoribe|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|Textile|en|Textile|WD=Q28823|lt=textiles}} under the {{ill|Bemin|en|Bemin|WD=Q2892644|lt=Be system}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|Cannabis|en|Cannabis|WD=Q79817|lt=hemp}} and {{ill|Paper mulberry|en|Paper mulberry|WD=Q389185|lt=paper mulberry}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|en|Sukune|WD=Q11455653|lt=Sukune}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684|en|684|WD=Q31128|lt=684 AD}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji}}, {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Omi}}, and {{ill|Neck|en|Neck|WD=Q9633|lt=Kashira}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} (compiled in {{ill|815|en|815|WD=Q29917|lt=815 AD}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province}}. revision.text:
[[ja:倭文氏]] [[ja:倭文氏]] [[ja:倭文氏]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|en|Sukune|WD=Q11455653|lt=Sukune}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōaji no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神|6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|lt=Heavenly Gods|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly and Earthly Gods|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=Clan}} that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|Uji (clan)|en|Uji (clan)|WD=Q2575910|lt=Uji}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitoribe|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|Textile|en|Textile|WD=Q28823|lt=textiles}} under the {{ill|Bemin|en|Bemin|WD=Q2892644|lt=Be system}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|Cannabis|en|Cannabis|WD=Q79817|lt=hemp}} and {{ill|Paper mulberry|en|Paper mulberry|WD=Q389185|lt=paper mulberry}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|en|Sukune|WD=Q11455653|lt=Sukune}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684|en|684|WD=Q31128|lt=684 AD}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji}}, {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Omi}}, and {{ill|Neck|en|Neck|WD=Q9633|lt=Kashira}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} (compiled in {{ill|815|en|815|WD=Q29917|lt=815 AD}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunogoromusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Arikiyo|1=Arikiyo Saeki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13364959}}|title={{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja|日本古代氏族事典【新装版】|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans (New Edition)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzankaku|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}|year=2015|isbn=9784639023791}} * {{citation|editor={{ill|Ueda Masahiro|ja|上田正昭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueda Masahiro|1=Masaaki Ueda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3297094}}, supervision by {{ill|Inoue Mitsuo|ja|井上満郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Mitsuo|1=Mitsuo Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16264281}} and {{ill|Atago Gen|ja|愛宕元|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Gen|1=Hajime Otagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11493824}}|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Yamato Shobo|ja|大和書房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Shobo|1=Daiwa Shobō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433473}}|year=2006|isbn=9784479840657}} * {{Cite web|url=https://yomiagaeru.exblog.jp/24181480/|author=夕暮れのKAKA|title={{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|work={{ill|Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja|蘇える出雲王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reviving the Izumo Dynasty|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=http://www.harimaya.com/o_kamon1/syake/south/s_sidori.html|author=播磨屋|title={{ill|The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja|社家の姓氏-倭文氏？-|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Family Name of the Shrine Family - Shitori Clan?|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|work={{ill|Family Crest World|ja|家紋World|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family Crest World|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} * {{Cite web|url=https://genbu.net/saijin/hazuti.htm|author=玄松子|title={{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}|work={{ill|Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja|玄松子の祭神記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshōshi’s Record of Deities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|accessdate=2020-01-22}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} == See Also == * Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōaji no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yamato)

An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitoribe|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|Textile|en|Textile|WD=Q28823|lt=textiles}} under the {{ill|Bemin|en|Bemin|WD=Q2892644|lt=Be system}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|Cannabis|en|Cannabis|WD=Q79817|lt=hemp}} and {{ill|Paper mulberry|en|Paper mulberry|WD=Q389185|lt=paper mulberry}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|en|Sukune|WD=Q11455653|lt=Sukune}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684|en|684|WD=Q31128|lt=684 AD}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji}}, {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Omi}}, and {{ill|Neck|en|Neck|WD=Q9633|lt=Kashira}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} (compiled in {{ill|815|en|815|WD=Q29917|lt=815 AD}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunogoromusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori revision.text:
[[ja:倭文氏]] [[ja:倭文氏]] [[ja:倭文氏]] {{Ancient Japanese Clans |name = {{ill|Shitori clan|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|lt=Shizuri clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}} |image = |surname = Shizuri no {{ill|Sukune|en|Sukune|WD=Q11455653|lt=Sukune|1=Sukune}} (Yamato, Kawachi)
Shizuri no {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji|1=Muraji}} (Settsu) |founder = {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}} |origin = |ancestor = {{ill|Ōaji no Sukune|ja|大味宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōaji no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Yamato)
{{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神|6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|lt=Heavenly Gods|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly and Earthly Gods|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=Clan|1=Clan}} that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|Uji (clan)|en|Uji (clan)|WD=Q2575910|lt=Uji|1=Uji (clan)}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitoribe|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|Textile|en|Textile|WD=Q28823|lt=textiles|1=Textile}} under the {{ill|Bemin|en|Bemin|WD=Q2892644|lt=Be system|1=Bemin}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|Cannabis|en|Cannabis|WD=Q79817|lt=hemp|1=Cannabis}} and {{ill|Paper mulberry|en|Paper mulberry|WD=Q389185|lt=paper mulberry|1=Paper mulberry}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji|1=Muraji}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|en|Sukune|WD=Q11455653|lt=Sukune|1=Sukune}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684|en|684|WD=Q31128|lt=684 AD|1=684}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji|1=Muraji}}, {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Omi|1=Omi (title)}}, and {{ill|Neck|en|Neck|WD=Q9633|lt=Kashira|1=Neck}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} (compiled in {{ill|815|en|815|WD=Q29917|lt=815 AD|1=815}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunogoromusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} remain across Japan. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{citation|editor={{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Arikiyo|1=Saeki Ariyoshi|comment=changed title based on {{ill|Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja|天伊佐布魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Settsu) |type = {{ill|Divine clan|ja|神別|1=Shinbetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|Heavenly and Earthly Gods|ja|天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神|6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|lt=Heavenly Gods|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly and Earthly Gods|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) |main_location = {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}, {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}}, and others |notable_figures = |descendants = }} Shitori clan (倭文氏, Shitori-uji, Shidori-uji, Shizuri-uji) was a {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|WD=Q211503|lt=Clan|1=Clan}} that bore the name "倭文" as its {{ill|Uji (clan)|en|Uji (clan)|WD=Q2575910|lt=Uji|1=Uji (clan)}}. An alternative spelling is "委文." == Overview == The Shitori clan was a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} lineage that led the {{ill|Shitoribe|ja|倭文部|1=Shizuri clan|2=ja|3=倭文氏|4=zh|5=倭文氏|6=de|7=Shizuri Clan|8=fr|9=Clan Shizuri|lt=Shizuri Bemin (vassals of the Shizuri clan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitoribe|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri clan|qq=}}, a group responsible for producing {{ill|Textile|en|Textile|WD=Q28823|lt=textiles|1=Textile}} under the {{ill|Bemin|en|Bemin|WD=Q2892644|lt=Be system|1=Bemin}}.{{ill|Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja|日本古代氏族事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しとり［委文］" The term "倭文" (Shitori) means *shizu-ori*, referring to an ancient Japanese textile woven with intricate patterns using fibers from plants such as {{ill|Cannabis|en|Cannabis|WD=Q79817|lt=hemp|1=Cannabis}} and {{ill|Paper mulberry|en|Paper mulberry|WD=Q389185|lt=paper mulberry|1=Paper mulberry}}.{{ill|Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja|日本古代史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Ancient Japanese History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - "しどり［倭文］" The central branch of the clan held the title {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji|1=Muraji}}, and its main line was granted the title {{ill|Sukune|en|Sukune|WD=Q11455653|lt=Sukune|1=Sukune}} in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmus reign ({{ill|684|en|684|WD=Q31128|lt=684 AD|1=684}}). In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji|1=Muraji}}, {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Omi|1=Omi (title)}}, and {{ill|Neck|en|Neck|WD=Q9633|lt=Kashira|1=Neck}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} (compiled in {{ill|815|en|815|WD=Q29917|lt=815 AD|1=815}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}}. Their origins are described as follows: Under "Divine Clans of Yamato Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Sukune descends from {{ill|Kamimusuhi|ja|神魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, and his descendant was Ōaji no Sukune." Meanwhile, under "Divine Clans of Settsu Province (Heavenly Gods)," the text states, "Itabumi no Muraji descends from {{ill|Tsunogoromusuhi|ja|角凝魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunogoromusuhi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and his son, Ama Isafutamashii no Mikoto."{{ill|Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja|倭文氏と下照姫 倭文神社（２）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Clan and Shitateruhime - Shitori Shrine (2)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} The Shitori clan enshrined {{ill|Takehazuchi|fr|Takehazuchi|lt=Amahazuchi-no-Kami|ja|天羽槌雄神|1=Takehazuchi|2=ja|3=天羽槌雄神|4=zh|5=天羽槌雄神|6=fr|7=Takehazuchi|lt=Takehazuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehazuchi|qq=}}, the deity of weaving, as their ancestral god, and many {{ill|Shizuri Shrines|ja|倭文神社|Shizuri Shrines=Shizuri Shrines|lt=Shitori Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuri Shrines|qq=}} remain across In regional areas, different branches of the clan held titles such as {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Muraji|1=Muraji}}, {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Omi|1=Omi (title)}}, and {{ill|Neck|en|Neck|WD=Q9633|lt=Kashira|1=Neck}}. According to the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} (compiled in {{ill|815|en|815|WD=Q29917|lt=815 AD|1=815}}), three branches of the Shitori clan were recorded: *Itabumi no Sukune* in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} and {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}, and *Itabumi no Muraji* in {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|WD=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}}.
